items.title: Hunter Henry: Remains top tight end
items.body_text: While Henry returns as the Patriots' top tight end, slotting behind him on the team's depth chart has yet to be solidified following Julian Hill's season-ending knee injury, Evan Lazar of the team's official site reports. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.title: Trevor Lawrence: Draws praise for offseason work
items.body_text: Lawrence has thrived in the early stages of his second offseason in head coach Liam Coen's system, John Shipley of SI.com reports. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.title: Christian McCaffrey: Wins Best Comeback Athlete
items.body_text: McCaffrey was named the Best Comeback Athlete at the 2026 ESPYS, Briana Jeannel of the 49ers' official site reports. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.title: Zay Flowers: Ravens keen on extension
items.body_text: Ravens general manager Eric DeCosta said Thursday on 105.7 The Fan that Baltimore "definitely" wants to ink Flowers to a contract extension before the start of the 2026 regular season, Cordell Woodland of 105.7 The Fan Baltimore reports. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.title: Alvin Kamara: Agrees to new contract with Saints
items.body_text: Kamara and New Orleans agreed to terms Thursday on a restructured contract that gives him a $6 million base salary for the 2026 season, Ian Rapoport of NFL Network reports. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.
